content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Enforcement Executive/Legal
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-4441

July 17, 1985

Alice Reimche, City Clerk
City Hall
221 W. Pine St.
Lodi, CA 95240

Re: Filing Officer's Duties to
Provide Campaign Disclosure
Forms and Information Manuals
to Filers
FPPC Advice No. A-85-158

Dear Alice:

You recently asked whether city filing officers are required to
provide campaign disclosure statements and informational manuals to
persons who are not required to file campaign statements with the
city. We have recently been asked the same question by a number of
city clerks who indicate that it is a considerable strain on the
city's budget to provide copies of these materials to everyone who
requests them. The problem most frequently arises because candidates
for county offices or for local district offices and committee
treasurers find it more convenient to obtain the needed materials
from the city clerk, rather than traveling to another location or
requesting the materials by mail from their own filing officer.

Gov. Code Section 81010 provides that:

With respect to reports and statements filed
with him pursuant to this title, the filing officer
shall:

(a) Supply the necessary forms and manuals
prescribed by the Commission;

. . . .

This provision means that a city clerk is required to supply
forms and informational materials to candidates and committee
treasurers who are required to file their campaign statements with
the city clerk. There is no requirement in the Political Reform Act
that filing officers provide forms and manuals to candidates and
treasurers who are not required to file with the filing officer.

Alice Reimche
July 17, 1985
Page 2


Therefore, it is permissible to advise candidates and treasurers
who are not required to file with the city to obtain the forms and
informational materials from their own filing officer.

Sincerely,

[illegible signature]

Jeanne Pritchard
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question: Whether city filing officers are required to provide campaign disclosure statements and informational manuals to persons who are not required to file campaign statements with the city.
sections.conclusion: There is no requirement in the Political Reform Act that filing officers provide forms and manuals to candidates and treasurers who are not required to file with the filing officer. Therefore, it is permissible to advise candidates and treasurers who are not required to file with the city to obtain the forms and informational materials from their own filing officer.
sections.question_synthetic: Are city filing officers obligated under the Political Reform Act to provide campaign disclosure forms and informational manuals to candidates and treasurers who are not required to file with that particular city?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: City filing officers are only required to provide campaign disclosure forms and manuals to those who must file with them under California Government Code Section 81010. Filing officers may direct other candidates and treasurers to obtain materials from their own filing officer.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document has clear question and conclusion sections. Minor OCR artifacts in header/letterhead do not affect extraction of substantive content. Advice number A-85-158 provided. Signed by Jeanne Pritchard, Political Reform Consultant.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether city filing officers are required to provide campaign disclosure statements and informational manuals to persons who are not required to file campaign statements with the city.

CONCLUSION: There is no requirement in the Political Reform Act that filing officers provide forms and manuals to candidates and treasurers who are not required to file with the filing officer. Therefore, it is permissible to advise candidates and treasurers who are not required to file with the city to obtain the forms and informational materials from their own filing officer.

We have recently been asked the same question by a number of city clerks who indicate that it is a considerable strain on the city's budget to provide copies of these materials to everyone who requests them. The problem most frequently arises because candidates for county offices or for local district offices and committee treasurers find it more convenient to obtain the needed materials from the city clerk, rather than traveling to another location or requesting the materials by mail from their own filing officer. embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter addresses whether city filing officers must provide campaign disclosure forms and manuals to all requesters or only to those required to file with them. The FPPC concludes that filing officers need only supply materials to those required to file with them, and may direct others to their own filing officer.
